Question: The voltage supplied to your house varies as $$V ( t ) = V_{\text{max}} \text{sin} ( 2 \pi f t )$$ . If a resistor is connected across this voltage, will Ohm’s law $$V = I R$$ still be valid?
Answer: Yes, Ohm’s law is still valid. At every point in time the current is equal to $$I ( t ) = V ( t ) \text{/} R$$ , so the current is also a function of time, $$I ( t ) = \frac{V_{\text{max}}}{R} \text{sin} ( 2 \pi f t )$$ .
